source_jobs.title: Account Lead, Social Commerce  | Talent Pool
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT DARKROOM

Darkroom is the leading next-generation growth marketing firm engineering the brands of tomorrow. Founded in 2017, we set out to redefine what a modern agency could be, by replacing the inertia of legacy advertising behemoths with a model built for speed, intelligence, and measurable impact.

At our core, Darkroom is a human services company powered by Shadow, a universal AI commerce layer that integrates executive-level strategy with proprietary agentic technology. This fusion enables our teams to deliver outsized returns by enhancing creative output, operational efficiency, and revenue generation across every stage of the customer journey.

Our track record speaks for itself: billions in attributable revenue driven across e-commerce marketplaces, media networks, DTC ecosystems, and social commerce platforms. Every engagement feeds into our proprietary data infrastructure, enabling a continuous feedback loop that accelerates growth, improves margins, and compounds results across our client portfolio.

What began as a boutique design studio has evolved into one of the fastest-growing private companies in America (Inc. 5000) and among the most effective performance media agencies of the 2020s (Varos). Our founders were recognized by Forbes 30 Under 30 for advancing the intersection of technology, marketing, and advertising, cementing Darkroom’s role as a defining player in the future of media innovation.






ABOUT THE ROLE

Please note: We are not actively hiring for this role at the moment, we are building a talent pool for future opportunities we anticipate opening soon. Strong candidates may be contacted proactively as our team continues to expand.



Darkroom is expanding its Social Commerce practice and seeking an Account Lead, TikTok Shop to manage client relationships, performance, and day-to-day execution for our growing portfolio of TikTok Shop brands.

As an Account Lead, you’ll be the primary partner for your brands—translating goals into actionable plans, coordinating cross-functional teams, and ensuring campaigns ship with precision. You’ll work closely with Strategists, Creator Managers, and Paid Media to deliver measurable GMV growth and operational excellence in a fast-moving, creator-driven commerce environment.

This role is ideal for someone who is highly organized, platform-savvy, client-driven, and ready to play a critical role in building the next-generation social commerce capability at Darkroom.






WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own Day-to-Day Client Management: Serve as the primary point of contact for TikTok Shop clients; maintain tight alignment on goals, performance, and account needs.

 - Drive Performance Execution: Partner with Strategy and Creator teams to manage product seeding, affiliate activation, campaign rollout, and in-platform optimizations.

 - Monitor & Translate Performance: Track GMV, campaign KPIs, and affiliate outputs; distill insights into clear next steps and escalate risks proactively.

 - Operational Command: Ensure all deliverables—creator briefs, product lists, content approvals, reporting—are shipped with accuracy and speed.

 - Creator & Platform Fluency: Leverage knowledge of TikTok Shop mechanics, creator dynamics, and algorithmic behavior to support smarter execution and client education.

 - Cross-Functional Coordination: Work closely with internal partners (Creative, Creator, Paid Media, Strategy) to ensure cohesive execution and strong client outcomes.

 - Account Expansion Support: Identify trends, gaps, and opportunities to help leadership create upsell pathways and strengthen account value.

 - Quality Control: Uphold Darkroom’s performance and communication standards across every client interaction and internal workflow.






YOU SHOULD APPLY IF THIS SOUNDS LIKE YOU

 - You have 2–4 years in account management, influencer/social commerce, or performance marketing roles.

 - You have hands-on TikTok Shop experience (minimum 6–12 months) managing or suppor

source_jobs.title: Designer, Social & Brand | Talent Pool
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT DARKROOM

Darkroom is the leading next-generation growth marketing firm, engineering the brands of tomorrow. Founded in 2017, we set out to redefine what a modern agency could be, by replacing the inertia of legacy advertising behemoths with a model built for speed, intelligence, and measurable impact.

At our core, Darkroom is a human services company powered by Shadow, a universal AI commerce layer that integrates executive-level strategy with proprietary agentic technology. This fusion enables our teams to deliver outsized returns by enhancing creative output, operational efficiency, and revenue generation across every stage of the customer journey.

Our track record speaks for itself: billions in attributable revenue driven across e-commerce marketplaces, media networks, DTC ecosystems, and social commerce platforms. Every engagement feeds into our proprietary data infrastructure, enabling a continuous feedback loop that accelerates growth, improves margins, and compounds results across our client portfolio.

What began as a boutique design studio has evolved into one of the fastest-growing private companies in America (Inc. 5000) and among the most effective performance media agencies of the 2020s (Varos). Our founders were recognized by Forbes 30 Under 30 for advancing the intersection of technology, marketing, and advertising, cementing Darkroom's role as a defining player in the future of media innovation.




ABOUT THE ROLE

This is a social content design role. You will own how Darkroom shows up visually on social media — carousels, static posts, content series, story templates, short-form video graphics, and campaign visuals across Instagram, LinkedIn, TikTok, and X.

You should understand what stops a scroll. You think about format constraints, platform behavior, and visual rhythm, not just aesthetics. You know that a carousel is a storytelling format, not a slide deck shrunk down. You know that a LinkedIn post image has a different job than an Instagram Reel cover.

You should also be a strong designer beyond social. You'll work on presentations, identity extensions, and marketing collateral.

You'll work closely with the VP, Marketing, and cross-functional partners to turn strategy and copy into design. This is a high-output role with real creative ownership — you won't just execute briefs. You'll concept visual systems, develop content series, and push the quality bar on everything that carries our name.



This is a fully remote role, EST hours (9 AM–5 PM).



Please note: We are not actively hiring for this role at the moment; we are building a talent pool for future opportunities we anticipate opening soon. Strong candidates may be contacted proactively as our team continues to expand.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

Social Content Design

 - Own the social content pipeline end-to-end: planning, narrative, storyboard, design, and iteration through approvals. Instagram is the priority platform, but you'll also design for LinkedIn, TikTok, and X.

 - Design carousels, static posts, story templates, short-form video graphics, and platform-native formats at a consistent weekly cadence. Carousels are a major format for Darkroom, and you should love making them.

 - Build visual systems and content series — repeatable frameworks and templates, not starting from scratch every time.

 - Hit content velocity targets. Consistency matters as much as quality.

 - Stay current on platform specs and in-feed trends and bring that into every asset.

 - Track performance and iterate. When something works, build on it. When it doesn't, diagnose and adjust.

 - Manage your own workstreams — flag blockers early and deliver on deadline.

Case Studies, Presentations, and Brand

 - Design case study visuals that turn client results into compelling proof points for sales and social.

 - Create assets for internal and external presentations: decks, pitch materials, and thought leadership visuals.

 - Maintain Darkroom's Figma-based brand s

source_jobs.title: Data and Automation Analyst - Associate
source_jobs.description:
DATA AND AUTOMATION ANALYST - ASSOCIATE


REMOTE (EST, 9 AM – 5 PM)




ABOUT DARKROOM

Darkroom is the leading next-generation growth marketing firm engineering the brands of tomorrow. Founded in 2017, we set out to redefine what a modern agency could be by replacing the inertia of legacy advertising behemoths with a model built for speed, intelligence, and measurable impact.

At our core, Darkroom is a human services company powered by Shadow, a universal AI commerce layer that integrates executive-level strategy with proprietary agentic technology. This fusion enables our teams to deliver outsized returns by enhancing creative output, operational efficiency, and revenue generation across every stage of the customer journey.

Our track record speaks for itself: billions in attributable revenue driven across e-commerce marketplaces, media networks, DTC ecosystems, and social commerce platforms. Every engagement feeds into our proprietary data infrastructure, enabling a continuous feedback loop that accelerates growth, improves margins, and compounds results across our client portfolio.

What began as a boutique design studio has evolved into one of the fastest-growing private companies in America (Inc. 5000) and among the most effective performance media agencies of the 2020s (Varos). Our founders were recognized by Forbes 30 Under 30 for advancing the intersection of technology, marketing, and advertising, cementing Darkroom's role as a defining player in the future of media innovation.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are hiring an Data and Automation Analyst - Associate to work across three areas: managing our proprietary Flight Plan infrastructure for e-commerce clients, designing and maintaining internal automations across our stack, and helping evaluate and deploy AI and emerging technology.

This is a high-ownership role for someone who thrives at the intersection of operational rigor, technical fluency, and client partnership. You will report to the VP of Finance and partner closely with operations, client service teams, and leadership.

This is a fully remote role supporting a team in the EST time zone (9 AM–5 PM EST). Hub locations include Brazil, Portugal and Spain.




WHAT YOU'LL DO


FLIGHT PLAN INFRASTRUCTURE

The Flight Plan is Darkroom's proprietary financial and operational infrastructure built specifically for e-commerce brands. It includes dashboard systems and P&L frameworks designed to give clients real-time visibility into their business performance. You will:

 - Own the full lifecycle of the Flight Plan from client onboarding through full operational deployment.

 - Ensure each client's Flight Plan is properly set up, maintained, and generating actionable insights.

 - Monitor the health and accuracy of each client's operational and financial dashboards — including P&Ls, revenue tracking, and margin reporting.

 - Proactively identify gaps or inefficiencies in a client's infrastructure and drive resolution.

 - Ensure clients are actively using and benefiting from the insights derived from their Flight Plan.


AUTOMATIONS & INTEGRATIONS

You design and deploy workflows across our internal stack (Zapier, Slack, Google Workspace, Fireflies, Notion, and more). You will:

 - Build and maintain automations that connect platforms and remove manual work.

 - Audit existing automations, catch weak points early, and keep everything running reliably.

 - Document workflows and integrations so the team can rely on them without you in the room.

 - Partner with cross-functional teams to surface pain points and design fit-for-purpose solutions.


AI & EMERGING TECHNOLOGY

You stay current on AI tools and operational technology trends. You will:

 - Experiment with LLMs and emerging tools and translate capability into operational value.

 - Help build AI-enhanced workflows that make the team faster and sharper.

 - Support leadership in evaluating new platforms and surface clear, well-scoped recommendations.

 - Document sys

source_jobs.title: Retention Specialist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT DARKROOM

Darkroom is the leading next-generation growth marketing firm engineering the brands of tomorrow. Founded in 2017, we set out to redefine what a modern agency could be, by replacing the inertia of legacy advertising behemoths with a model built for speed, intelligence, and measurable impact.

This fusion enables our teams to deliver outsized returns by enhancing creative output, operational efficiency, and revenue generation across every stage of the customer journey.

Our track record speaks for itself: billions in attributable revenue driven across e-commerce marketplaces, media networks, DTC ecosystems, and social commerce platforms. Every engagement feeds into our proprietary data infrastructure, enabling a continuous feedback loop that accelerates growth, improves margins, and compounds results across our client portfolio.

What began as a boutique design studio has evolved into one of the fastest-growing private companies in America (Inc. 5000) and among the most effective performance media agencies of the 2020s (Varos). Our founders were recognized by Forbes 30 Under 30 for advancing the intersection of technology, marketing, and advertising, cementing Darkroom’s role as a defining player in the future of media innovation.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are hiring a Retention Specialist — a strategic, hands-on lifecycle marketer with a proven track record in email and SMS for DTC brands. You'll partner closely with Associate Directors of Retention and cross-functional teams to develop and deploy high-performing campaigns for some of the fastest-growing DTC brands in the world — translating data into strategy and strategy into revenue.

Darkroom is a career accelerator for marketers who want to operate at the intersection of creativity, commerce, and performance - while mastering the tools and tactics of modern CRM strategy.



This is a fully remote role supporting a team in the EST time zone and is open to candidates based in Brazil, Portugal, and Spain.




A DAY IN THE LIFE

 - Lead the execution of email and SMS strategies for a portfolio of DTC/e-commerce clients, working alongside our Associate Directors of Retention to align on goals and priorities.

 - Own the end-to-end campaign deployment process - copywriting, briefing, testing, QA, deployment, and performance monitoring.

 - Contribute to monthly retention marketing calendars and proactively bring creative campaign concepts to clients and internal teams.

 - Lead portions of client calls - presenting content ideas and managing the revision and approval process with confidence.

 - Partner with the in-house design team to shape the creative direction of campaigns and flows, ensuring visual and copy are working together to drive results.

 - Build, audit, and optimize automation flows, pop-up logic, segmentation strategies, and app integrations.

 - Analyze campaign and flow performance and surface actionable recommendations that improve retention KPIs over time.




WHO YOU ARE:

We're looking for a Retention Specialist to join our team! In this role, you will write email and SMS marketing copy, build and deploy email and SMS flows and campaigns, and develop content for our e-commerce, DTC clients.

 - You have 3-5 years of experience in email and SMS marketing, lifecycle marketing, or CRM — ideally at a DTC-focused agency or in-house at an e-commerce brand.

 - You're a Klaviyo power user — flows, segmentation, A/B testing, deliverability, and reporting are all in your toolkit.

 - You're a strong copywriter who can adapt tone and voice across different brands and audiences.

 - You think strategically about the customer journey and know how to use retention levers to drive LTV and reduce churn.

 - You’re client-facing and comfortable - you can present a strategy and manage f

source_jobs.title: Associate Director, Design - Talent Pool
source_jobs.description:

Our founders were recognized by Forbes 30 Under 30 for advancing the intersection of technology, marketing, and advertising, cementing Darkroom’s role as a defining player in the future of media innovation.



About the role

We are hiring an Associate Director, Design- a visionary leader with a strong design background who thrives in both managing creative talent and driving high-quality design execution across various platforms. You’ll combine strategic oversight with hands-on involvement, ensuring that every design project meets the highest standards of excellence.

You will report to the Director of Creative and play a key role in guiding a talented team of designers, overseeing the delivery of exceptional designs for paid ads, emails, landing pages, and e-commerce web design. With your deep expertise in UI/UX and brand design, you’ll empower your team to elevate their skills while maintaining a high bar for creative output and attention to detail.

This role requires a balance of leadership and creativity in a fast-paced, collaborative environment, where you’ll work closely with marketing, product, and development teams to ensure the alignment of design goals with broader business objectives. Your ability to drive creative strategy and manage multiple projects with precision will be essential to your success. Darkroom is a career incubator for ambitious talent looking to operate at the intersection of business, design, and innovation.

This is a fully remote role operating in the EST Time Zone and all business is conducted 100% in English.



Please note: We are not actively hiring for this role at the moment, we are building a talent pool for future opportunities we anticipate opening soon. Strong candidates may be contacted proactively as our team continues to expand.



Management Responsibilities:

 - Manage a team of designers, starting with 2-4 direct reports and expanding as the team grows. Provide support in strategy, project management, design feedback, performance evaluation, and handling escalations.

 - Conduct weekly 1:1s with direct reports to assess client health, offer feedback, and oversee career development.

 - Ensure high-quality work output from direct reports, focusing on visual craft, UX expertise, and meeting deadlines.

 - Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including marketers, strategists, developers, and clients, to develop project plans, understand business objectives, and address client issues.

 - Refine internal processes for client work in coordination with the Director of Creative. Provide training and feedback to enhance the skills of the design team.

Senior Individual Contributor Responsibilities:

 - Lead 1-2 UI/UX engagements as needed, focusing on creating branded e

source_jobs.title: Associate Director, Creative Strategy (Paid)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT DARKROOM

Darkroom is the leading next-generation growth marketing firm engineering the brands of tomorrow. Founded in 2017, we set out to redefine what a modern agency could be, by replacing the inertia of legacy advertising behemoths with a model built for speed, intelligence, and measurable impact.

At our core, Darkroom is a human services company powered by Shadow, a universal AI commerce layer that integrates executive-level strategy with proprietary agentic technology. This fusion enables our teams to deliver outsized returns by enhancing creative output, operational efficiency, and revenue generation across every stage of the customer journey.

Our track record speaks for itself: billions in attributable revenue driven across e-commerce marketplaces, media networks, DTC ecosystems, and social commerce platforms. Every engagement feeds into our proprietary data infrastructure, enabling a continuous feedback loop that accelerates growth, improves margins, and compounds results across our client portfolio.

What began as a boutique design studio has evolved into one of the fastest-growing private companies in America (Inc. 5000) and among the most effective performance media agencies of the 2020s (Varos). Our founders were recognized by Forbes 30 Under 30 for advancing the intersection of technology, marketing, and advertising, cementing Darkroom’s role as a defining player in the future of media innovation.




ABOUT THE ROLE

This is a fully remote role supporting a team in the EST time zone (9 AM–5 PM EST), with all business conducted 100% in English.

We’re looking for a visionary creative leader with deep experience in developing ad concepts for digital paid media advertising formats on Meta, TikTok, and other channels. You’re able to dive deep into the pixels and give tactical feedback on design and video, but understand how to thread the needle between performance frameworks, creative testing, and world-building assets. You’re likely a Creative Strategist by trade with a background in Design, Video Editing, or Performance Marketing. You are hyper-focused on paid media performance and developing advertisements that sell products and services.

You'll work on our Performance Creative service—our Al-powered creative strategy engagement for paid media clients that want to work with us on the following:

 - Expertise: Engage us for our expertise in creative strategy, which includes assessing the efficacy of their ad hooks, ad bodies, and general creative direction. You will have a clear perspective on how to improve this over time.

 - Sales: Deliver game changing creative ads that sell their products and services better than anyone else.

 - Diversity: Increase the creative diversity of their ad program through the use of multiple ad formats: statics, motion, video, UGC, AI.

 - Volume: Increase the volume of their performance creative program by delivering upwards of 50-100 new ads per month with the intention of scaling that as needed.

This role is for individuals who want to be at the forefront of digital advertising, AI in marketing, and join a team that is building the best creative advertising culture in the business. You will work directly on our Creative Team, which works with our clients to enhance their Creative Output. You will also work with both our Social Commerce team to enhance UGC creative and our Paid Media team to plug into their ad scaling strategies. Generally, your work will be the lifeblood of our ad programs.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives at the intersection of creativity, AI, and performance - combining bold visual storytelling with data-informed design decisions. You'll report to the Director of Creative and lead the management of Designers and Editors to execute paid media assets across a variety of formats, including static ads, short-form videos, motion graphics, and UGC content. Your work will directly impact client performance by delivering a high-volume of ad concept

source_jobs.title: AI Marketing Specialist (SEO Specialist) - Talent Pool
source_jobs.description:
About Darkroom

Darkroom is the leading next-generation growth marketing firm engineering the brands of tomorrow. Founded in 2017, we set out to redefine what a modern agency could be, by replacing the inertia of legacy advertising behemoths with a model built for speed, intelligence, and measurable impact.

At our core, Darkroom is a human services company powered by Matter, a universal AI commerce layer that integrates executive-level strategy with proprietary agentic technology. This fusion enables our teams to deliver outsized returns by enhancing creative output, operational efficiency, and revenue generation across every stage of the customer journey.

Our track record speaks for itself: billions in attributable revenue driven across e-commerce marketplaces, media networks, DTC ecosystems, and social commerce platforms. What began as a boutique design studio has evolved into one of the fastest-growing private companies in America (Inc. 5000) and among the most effective performance media agencies of the 2020s (Varos). Our founders were recognized by Forbes 30 Under 30 for advancing the intersection of technology, marketing, and advertising.




ABOUT THE ROLE

The way consumers discover, evaluate, and choose brands is being rewritten in real time. LLM-powered search, AI agents, and generative experiences are replacing the decade-old playbook of keywords and blue links. Darkroom is building a new AI Marketing division to own this space, and we need an SEO Specialist who can operate at the intersection of traditional organic search and the emerging world of AI-mediated discovery.

As an SEO Specialist within the AI Marketing division, you will manage all aspects of SEO and GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) strategy and execution for a portfolio of high-growth consumer brands. This is not a traditional SEO role. You will be responsible for ensuring that Darkroom clients are discoverable, recommended, and cited by both traditional search engines and LLM-powered platforms like ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews, and Gemini.

You will report to the Associate Director of AI Marketing and work closely with content, creative, paid media, and data teams to deliver integrated organic growth strategies. This is a fast-paced, high-accountability environment for someone who is curious about AI, obsessed with search, and ready to help define a category that is still being written.

Please note: We are not actively hiring for this role at the moment, we are building a talent pool for future opportunities we anticipate opening soon. Strong candidates may be contacted proactively as our team continues to expand.




WHAT YOU’LL DO




SEO STRATEGY & EXECUTION

 - Develop and implement comprehensive SEO strategies aligned with client business objectives across e-commerce, DTC, and consumer brands.

 - Conduct keyword research, site audits, and competitor analyses to identify growth opportunities in both traditional and AI-powered search environments.

 - Continuously analyze and report on SEO performance metrics, providing actionable insights and clear recommendations to clients and internal stakeholders.

 - Monitor rankings, organic traffic, and AI citation frequency, adjusting tactics based on performance data.


AI SEARCH & GENERATIVE ENGINE OPTIMIZATION (GEO)

 - Optimize client content and digital properties for visibility within LLM-powered search engines (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews, Gemini, Claude).

 - Develop and execute strategies to increase how often and how favorably client brands are referenced in AI-generated answers and recommendations.

 - Monitor the rapidly shifting landscape of how LLMs source, rank, and cite information, and translate changes into actionable client strategies.

 - Build and maintain frameworks for tracking “AI share of voice” across generative search platforms.


ON-PAGE & TECHNICAL SEO

 - Optimize website content, meta tags, structured data, and internal linking structures to

source_jobs.title: Full-Stack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHAT WE'RE BUILDING

We're empowering small teams with technology that makes it easier to market and grow businesses. Our current focus is to help consumer brands shift from "workflow automation" to "agent management" within their marketing operations. Shadow is the AI coordination layer — providing shared AI memory, centralized agent control, and model orchestration for marketing teams.

 


WHY JOIN SHADOW?

 - Product Ownership You'll ship production code daily and help steer key product and technical decisions.

 - Shape the Engineering Culture You'll influence how we work—tools, processes, standards, and hiring.

 - Work with Challenger Consumer Brands Talk directly to customers (CEOs, CMOs, VP's) of fast-growing consumer brands—some doing $80M–$500M in revenue.

 


THE AGENCY BEHIND THE PRODUCT

Shadow is built alongside Darkroom — a performance marketing agency that's been operating for 10 years, employs 100+ people, runs 100+ clients at a time, and has worked with over 1,000 consumer brands. The agency is both our proving ground and our first user, which means the data you build with is real marketing data at real volume from day one — not a synthetic demo.



This is a fully remote role supporting a team in the EST time zone (9 AM–5 PM EST).




THE ROLE

You'll sit squarely at the intersection of back‑end and front‑end, ensuring seamless integration between APIs, databases, UIs, and ML services. You'll design, build, and scale features end‑to‑end, especially our AI/ML‑powered experiences, while mentoring peers and driving architecture decisions.



Core Tech & Tools

 - Languages & Frameworks: Python, Node.js, React (TypeScript)

 - Datastore: PostgreSQL

 - Cloud & Infra: Google Cloud Platform, Airflow, Terraform, Docker, Kubernetes

 - ML/AI: LLMs, RAG, prompt engineering

 


WHAT YOU'LL OWN

 - Architect and implement full‑stack features, from database schema to React components, optimized for scale and reliability.

 - Build and maintain RESTful/GraphQL APIs, data pipelines, and distributed services in GCP.

 - Integrate, prompt, and debug LLMs and generative AI tools; own RAG or fine‑tuning pipelines.

 - Ensure front‑end and back‑end systems interoperate flawlessly, minimize friction, optimize data flow, and enforce contracts.

 - Collaborate with product, research, design, and infra teams to define requirements, iterate rapidly, and ship production‑grade code.

 - Monitor performance, reliability, and security.

 - Mentor junior engineers through code reviews, architecture reviews, and shared best practices.

 


MUST HAVES

 - 5+ years of professional software engineering experience with end‑to‑end ownership in a full‑stack role.

 - Deep expertise in Python, Node.js, React/TypeScript, and PostgreSQL.

 - Able to be hands‑on with GCP, containerization (Docker/K8s), and building/supporting high‑traffic systems.

 - Proven experience integrating AI/ML models (LLMs, NLP, RAG) into production apps.

 - Familiarity or strong interest in working with MCP servers.

 - Exceptional problem‑solving skills and a product mindset: you think deeply about UX, performance, and business impact.

 - You sweat both technical details and end-user experience.

 


NICE TO HAVES

 - Experience with multi‑step or agentic AI workflows.

 - Background in AI infrastructure or tooling companies.

 - Contributions to open‑source AI/ML projects.

 


WHAT WE OFFER

 - Unlimited PTO + Local holidays (Relevant to your hub): Rebooting is part of the work. Take the time you need to stay sharp.

 - Remote-First Culture: Many roles are fully remote. Employees based in or near our New York or Lisbon HQs are expected to work hybrid with weekly in-office time. Hub locations include Brazil and Spain.

 - Parental Leave: Flexible parental leave to support new parents during this important transition.

 - Growth: Our interdisciplinary model gives every employee exposure far beyond their core role. Grow your skills, expand your influence, and stay

source_jobs.title: Director, Creative Strategy (Organic Social)
source_jobs.description:

Our founders were recognized by Forbes 30 Under 30 for advancing the intersection of technology, marketing, and advertising, cementing Darkroom’s role as a defining player in the future of media innovation.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for a visionary creative leader with deep experience building platform-native content strategies for organic social, primarily Instagram and TikTok, with fluency across other channels. You're able to dive deep into the feed and give tactical feedback on statics, hooks, pacing, captions, and edit structure, but understand how to thread the needle between brand world-building, content systems, and organic distribution. You're likely a Social or Creative Strategist by trade with a background in Content Creation, Video Editing, or Community-Led Growth. You are hyper-focused on earned attention and building owned channels that compound into audience, affinity, and revenue.

You'll work on our Organic Social and/or Performance Creative service, our AI-powered creative engagement for brands that want to work with us on the following:

 - Expertise: Engage us for our expertise in organic social, which includes building content strategies, creative direction, and building an organic social engine that audiences want to follow and connect with.

 - Engagement: Deliver game-changing creative that builds brand worlds and sells products and services better than anyone else.

 - Diversity: Increase the creative diversity of their social or ad program through the use of multiple ad formats: statics, motion, video, UGC, AI.

 - Volume: Increase the volume of their creative program by delivering upwards of 50+ new creatives per month with the intention of scaling that as needed.

This role is for individuals who want to be at the forefront of social-first creative, AI in marketing, and join a team that is building the best creative content culture in the business. You will work directly on our Creative Team to output content. You will also work with our Social Commerce team to brief creator and UGC content, and our Paid Media team to identify organic winners worth scaling into paid. Generally, your work will be the front door to each brand we represent.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives at the intersection of creativity, AI, and platform fluency, combining bold visual storytelling with data-informed content decisions. You'll report to the Director of Creative and lead Designers and Editors to execute organic assets across formats, including statics, GIFs, short-form video, carousels, motion graphics, Stories, and creator content. Your work will directly impact client growth by delivering a high volume of native content, elevated creative direction, and data-backed content frameworks.

source_jobs.title: Paid Social Manager - Talent Pool
source_jobs.description:
About Darkroom

Darkroom is the leading next-generation growth marketing firm engineering the brands of tomorrow. Founded in 2017, we set out to redefine what a modern agency could be, by replacing the inertia of legacy advertising behemoths with a model built for speed, intelligence, and measurable impact.

At our core, Darkroom is a human services company powered by Shadow, a universal AI commerce layer that integrates executive-level strategy with proprietary agentic technology. This fusion enables our teams to deliver outsized returns by enhancing creative output, operational efficiency, and revenue generation across every stage of the customer journey.

Our track record speaks for itself: billions in attributable revenue driven across e-commerce marketplaces, media networks, DTC ecosystems, and social commerce platforms. Every engagement feeds into our proprietary data infrastructure, enabling a continuous feedback loop that accelerates growth, improves margins, and compounds results across our client portfolio.

What began as a boutique design studio has evolved into one of the fastest-growing private companies in America (Inc. 5000) and among the most effective performance media agencies of the 2020s (Varos). Our founders were recognized by Forbes 30 Under 30 for advancing the intersection of technology, marketing, and advertising, cementing Darkroom's role as a defining player in the future of media innovation.



About the Role

We’re hiring a Paid Social Manager to lead performance-driven campaigns across platforms like Meta, TikTok, Pinterest, and CTV. This role goes beyond execution, combining creative strategy, campaign management, and data analysis to drive results at scale.

You’ll own the full lifecycle of paid social campaigns, from building and optimizing to scaling accounts often exceeding $1M/month, while working closely with creative, brand, and analytics teams. We’re looking for someone deeply experienced in Meta direct response advertising, with a strong track record of selling products through paid social and a sharp understanding of short-form video creative.

This is a fast-paced, high-accountability environment where you’ll collaborate directly with leadership to grow high-performing U.S. brands. Darkroom is a career incubator for ambitious marketers operating at the intersection of business, design, and innovation.

Please note that we’re not actively hiring for this role at the moment. Instead, we’re building a talent pool for upcoming opportunities, and we may reach out to strong candidates as our team continues to expand.



What you'll do:

 - Plan, develop, and implement comprehensive paid media strategies primarily focused on Paid Social to increase brand visibility and recognition and drive new user acquisition and leads via paid media campaigns.

 - Conduct thorough brand and website audits to understand our clients’ key value propositions, core products, and brand aesthetics and identify opportunities to successfully market them and make them stand out in the marketplace.

 - Obsession with short form creative and direct response creative to convert customers — how to best sell a product, how to best construct a hook, how to best guide a client on how their advertising dollars should be spent behind winning creatives.

 - Conduct competitive research to ensure success in the ad space and new acquisition audience targeting.

 - Manage between 5-10 Paid Media accounts (including but not limited to: Meta Ads, TikTok Pinterest, and other social platforms like Twitter and TikTok) on behalf of Darkroom clients, with aggregate monthly spends ranging between $80,000 - $3M.

 - Utilize Platform Ad Managers, third-party reporting tools, andAI to develop insights and action items intended to increase our clients’ revenues month-over-month and solve for attribution.

 - Religious mastery of important KPIs like CPA, CAC, iROAS, nCAC, MER, aMER, CTR, TSR, and Hold Rate.

 - Understand industry-wide bench

source_jobs.title: Senior Designer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT DARKROOM

Darkroom is the leading next-generation growth marketing firm engineering the brands of tomorrow. Founded in 2017, we set out to redefine what a modern agency could be, by replacing the inertia of legacy advertising behemoths with a model built for speed, intelligence, and measurable impact.

At our core, Darkroom is a human services company powered by Shadow, a universal AI commerce layer that integrates executive-level strategy with proprietary agentic technology. This fusion enables our teams to deliver outsized returns by enhancing creative output, operational efficiency, and revenue generation across every stage of the customer journey.

Our track record speaks for itself: billions in attributable revenue driven across e-commerce marketplaces, media networks, DTC ecosystems, and social commerce platforms. Every engagement feeds into our proprietary data infrastructure, enabling a continuous feedback loop that accelerates growth, improves margins, and compounds results across our client portfolio.

What began as a boutique design studio has evolved into one of the fastest-growing private companies in America (Inc. 5000) and among the most effective performance media agencies of the 2020s (Varos). Our founders were recognized by Forbes 30 Under 30 for advancing the intersection of technology, marketing, and advertising, cementing Darkroom’s role as a defining player in the future of media innovation.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Darkroom is looking for a Senior Designer who’s ready to roll up their sleeves and dive deep into brand, performance, and product design. We’re looking for more than just execution, we’re looking for perspective. You ask smart questions, push creative thinking, and know how to bring ideas to life in a way that’s both strategic and visually sharp.

This role is for someone confident leading creative projects across a range of deliverables, from landing pages and retention flows to paid ads and digital branding, while staying grounded in strategy and results.

You’ll work closely with Retention Managers, Developers, and Designers to deliver polished work that balances brand and performance. You’ll lead initiatives independently while collaborating often, and communicate directly with clients, presenting designs, leading workshops, and managing approvals.

You’ll report to our Director of Creative and work directly with fast-growing brands where the stakes and opportunities are high. This role is designed for someone ready to take on more ownership, broaden their skills across disciplines, and grow quickly within a team that values both creativity and clarity.

This is a fully remote role supporting a team in the EST time zone.

What You’ll Do

 - Design digital assets that support a range of client marketing initiatives briefed by a Marketing Manager: paid ads, email & SMS, landing pages, organic social, Amazon creative, and more.

 - Create design systems based on a client’s brand guidelines to be used across multiple channels, ensuring consistency of a client’s brand identity, look, and feel.

 - Communicate with clients directly and present designs on calls, lead clients through workshopping, and obtain approvals while maintaining a strong working relationship.

 - Develop a deep understanding of what factors drive customers to convert on different marketing channels and execute designs within established best practices.

 - Collaborate closely with team members and clients for feedback and approvals to ensure your work adheres to a high standard of craft.

 - Design landing pages within Figma by using UI/UX best practices and adhering to Darkroom’s web design guidelines.

 - Collaborate with project managers and developers to deliver web design initiatives such as landing pages, web design updates, and more.

 - This role is on the Creative team and reports to the Design Director. You’ll work alongside a team of 8+ Designers, each supporting their own roster of 5+ client accounts.

source_jobs.title: Creator Manager | Talent Pool
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT DARKROOM

Darkroom is the leading next-generation growth marketing firm engineering the brands of tomorrow. Founded in 2017, we set out to redefine what a modern agency could be, by replacing the inertia of legacy advertising behemoths with a model built for speed, intelligence, and measurable impact.

At our core, Darkroom is a human services company powered by Shadow, a universal AI commerce layer that integrates executive-level strategy with proprietary agentic technology. This fusion enables our teams to deliver outsized returns by enhancing creative output, operational efficiency, and revenue generation across every stage of the customer journey.

Our track record speaks for itself: billions in attributable revenue driven across e-commerce marketplaces, media networks, DTC ecosystems, and social commerce platforms. Every engagement feeds into our proprietary data infrastructure, enabling a continuous feedback loop that accelerates growth, improves margins, and compounds results across our client portfolio.

What began as a boutique design studio has evolved into one of the fastest-growing private companies in America (Inc. 5000) and among the most effective performance media agencies of the 2020s (Varos). Our founders were recognized by Forbes 30 Under 30 for advancing the intersection of technology, marketing, and advertising, cementing Darkroom’s role as a defining player in the future of media innovation.






ABOUT THE ROLE

Please note: We are not actively hiring for this role at the moment, we are building a talent pool for future opportunities we anticipate opening soon. Strong candidates may be contacted proactively as our team continues to expand.



Darkroom is hiring a Creator Manager to lead client-facing creator strategy and execution across our Creator Services division. This role owns the relationship between brands and our creator network - ensuring campaigns are strategically aligned, operationally sound, and performance-driven across platforms, including TikTok Shop, Amazon affiliate, LTK, YouTube, and other emerging commerce channels.

As a Creator Manager, you will serve as the primary point of contact for both clients and creators. You will shape activation strategy, oversee execution, and ensure creator output drives measurable impact while maintaining a strong creator experience. If you thrive at the intersection of relationships, strategy, and commerce - this role was built for you.

This is a fully remote role supporting a team in the EST time zone (9 AM–5 PM EST).






WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Creator Strategy & Client Leadership: Lead creator and influencer strategy across client accounts, serving as the primary client-facing point of contact - presenting strategy, performance insights, and optimization recommendations.

 - Campaign Execution: Own end-to-end execution of creator campaigns, including sourcing, vetting, contracting, onboarding, and performance tracking.

 - Platform Activation: Activate creators across TikTok Shop, affiliate programs, paid UGC, Amazon, LTK, YouTube, and other emerging channels.

 - Commerce & Creative Alignment: Partner with the Account team and Content Studio to align creator efforts to hero SKUs, commission strategy, and creative frameworks.

 - Performance Monitoring: Track campaign performance and proactively recommend optimization opportunities.

 - Network & Operations: Manage usage rights, timelines, deliverables, CRM systems, and Darkroom's creator network infrastructure.

 - Team Development: Mentor junior team members and help scale best practices within Creator Services.






YOU SHOULD APPLY IF THIS SOUNDS LIKE YOU

 - 3–6 years of experience in influencer marketing, creator partnerships, UGC, or affiliate programs.

 - Strong client management experience and comfort leading strategy conversations.

 - Deep understanding of creator workflows, usage rights, and cross-platform activation.

 - Experience with TikTok Shop and affiliate models preferred;

source_jobs.title: Forward-Deployed Marketer
source_jobs.description:
WHAT WE'RE BUILDING

We're empowering small teams with technology that makes it easier to market and grow businesses. Our current focus is to help consumer brands shift from "workflow automation" to "agent management" within their marketing operations. Shadow is the AI coordination layer — providing shared AI memory, centralized agent control, and model orchestration for marketing teams.




WHY JOIN SHADOW?

 - Product Ownership You'll ship production code daily and help steer key product and technical decisions.

 - Shape the Engineering Culture You'll influence how we work—tools, processes, standards, and hiring.

 - Work with Challenger Consumer Brands Talk directly to customers (CEOs, CMOs, VP's) of fast-growing consumer brands—some doing $80M–$500M in revenue.




THE AGENCY BEHIND THE PRODUCT

Shadow is built alongside Darkroom — a performance marketing agency that's been operating for 10 years, employs 100+ people, runs 100+ clients at a time, and has worked with over 1,000 consumer brands. That's our edge: Shadow isn't a generic AI wrapper, it's a decade of real campaign tradecraft being codified into a system. Darkroom is both our proving ground and our first user. This role plugs directly into that knowledge and turns it into product.




THE ROLE

Part senior growth marketer, part data scientist, part applied-AI builder — you turn the way elite marketers think into the data models, metrics, and schemas that power Shadow's intelligence layer. You report directly to the CEO of Shadow.

This is for someone who's spent years in the work and now wants to lean into the technology — leveraging hard-won marketing experience to build, not to manage accounts. This is not a client-facing role.




WHAT YOU'LL OWN

 - Design the analytical models and metric logic the agent reasons with — contribution margin (CM3), acquisition truth (aMER, NCAC), cohort LTV/payback, ad spend efficiency and marginal-return analysis, incrementality testing (geo lifts, conversion-lift, MMM calibration) — from raw platform data to decision-ready insight.

 - Define the schemas that encode marketing tradecraft: how creative, channel, financial, and customer data connect into a queryable picture of a brand.

 - Own accuracy and judgment — what's load-bearing vs. noise, where attribution lies, how to compute metrics that survive operator scrutiny.

 - Spec the model; partner with data eng to build the pipeline and the AI team to wire it into agent skills.




MUST HAVE

 - Ran growth at one or more high-growth DTC / omni-channel consumer brands — you've managed paid media tactically, not just supervised people who did.

 - Fluency across the full marketing mix (Meta + Google, plus TikTok, email/SMS, marketplace, organic) — you think in MER/CM/LTV, not platform ROAS.

 - Real data science chops: SQL + Python/notebooks, statistical reasoning, building and validating metric models against messy real-world data.

 - Ability to translate between marketer intuition and rigorous structure — and a strong opinion about which metrics actually matter.




NICE TO HAVE

 - Familiarity with modern warehouse/analytics stacks (BigQuery, dbt) — enough to design schemas and collaborate with eng.

 - Agency or multi-brand background (pattern recognition across accounts).

 - Built attribution models, forecasting/MMM, or internal analytics dashboards.




CULTURE FIT

 - You’re a power AI user. You've embedded AI into every workflow you touch and you think in systems — not one-off prompts, but repeatable structures that compound.

 - Entrepreneurial. You don't need much direction to move fast, you pivot when the situation demands it, and what you ship is production-grade, not a prototype you hand off for someone else to finish.




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Competitive salary (roles, responsibilities, and comp grow as we do)

 - Top-tier health, vision, dental insurance (US)

 - Regular team off-sites

 - Regular hack weeks




COMPENSATION

Yearly compensation for this role is

source_jobs.description:

Darkroom is the leading next-generation growth marketing firm engineering the brands of tomorrow. Founded in 2017, we set out to redefine what a modern agency could be, by replacing the inertia of legacy advertising behemoths with a model built for speed, intelligence, and measurable impact.

At our core, Darkroom is a human services company powered by Shadow, a universal AI commerce layer that integrates executive-level strategy with proprietary agentic technology. This fusion enables our teams to deliver outsized returns by enhancing creative output, operational efficiency, and revenue generation across every stage of the customer journey.

Our track record speaks for itself: billions in attributable revenue driven across e-commerce marketplaces, media networks, DTC ecosystems, and social commerce platforms. Every engagement feeds into our proprietary data infrastructure, enabling a continuous feedback loop that accelerates growth, improves margins, and compounds results across our client portfolio.

What began as a boutique design studio has evolved into one of the fastest-growing private companies in America (Inc. 5000) and among the most effective performance media agencies of the 2020s (Varos). Our founders were recognized by Forbes 30 Under 30 for advancing the intersection of technology, marketing, and advertising, cementing Darkroom’s role as a defining player in the future of media innovation.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are hiring for our Paid Media team, looking for an Associate Director, Paid Social — a senior performance leader who combines deep platform expertise with strategic vision, team leadership, and executive-level client management. This is not a tactical execution role; it demands the ability to architect full-funnel paid social strategies, lead a team of practitioners, and drive measurable revenue outcomes across a portfolio of high-growth brands.

The ideal candidate is a recognized expert in Meta direct response advertising with a proven track record of scaling ad accounts well beyond $1M USD per month, building and mentoring high-performing teams, and serving as a trusted strategic advisor to senior client stakeholders. You think in systems, not campaigns — and you hold yourself accountable to the business outcomes that matter.

You'll report to the Managing Director of the department and serve as a senior leader within the Paid Media practice, owning the strategy, performance, and growth of a portfolio of key accounts. You'll collaborate closely with Creative, Analytics, Brand, and Client Strategy teams, and will be responsible for developing the next generation of paid social talent at Darkroom.

This is a fast-paced, high-accountability environment designed for marketers who are ambitious, curious, and ready to grow. You’ll gain hands-on experience in a broad range of platforms and verticals, while developing the skills needed to lead performance strategies end-to-end.

Darkroom is a career incubator for ambitious talent looking to operate at the intersection of business, design, and innovation.




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Own and drive the overarching paid social strategy across a portfolio of high-growth U.S. brands, ensuring alignment between media investment, creative output, and business objectives.

 - Serve as the executive point of contact for senior client stakeholders — presenting quarterly business reviews, growth roadmaps, and performance forecasts to CMOs and marketing leadership.

 - Partner with the Managing Director to define team OKRs, capacity planning, and service delivery standards across the Paid Social practice.

 - Champion Darkroom's paid social point of view, staying ahead of platform changes, algorithmic shifts, and emerging formats to inform both client strategy and internal best practices.

 - Lead the development of annual and quarterly paid media plans, including budget allocation, channel mix, audience strategy, and creative roadmaps.

 - Oversee campaign arc

source_jobs.title: Shopify Full Stack Developer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT DARKROOM

Darkroom is the leading next-generation growth marketing firm engineering the brands of tomorrow. Founded in 2017, we set out to redefine what a modern agency could be, by replacing the inertia of legacy advertising behemoths with a model built for speed, intelligence, and measurable impact.

At our core, Darkroom is a human services company powered by Shadow, a universal AI commerce layer that integrates executive-level strategy with proprietary agentic technology. This fusion enables our teams to deliver outsized returns by enhancing creative output, operational efficiency, and revenue generation across every stage of the customer journey.

Our track record speaks for itself: billions in attributable revenue driven across e-commerce marketplaces, media networks, DTC ecosystems, and social commerce platforms. Every engagement feeds into our proprietary data infrastructure, enabling a continuous feedback loop that accelerates growth, improves margins, and compounds results across our client portfolio.

What began as a boutique design studio has evolved into one of the fastest-growing private companies in America (Inc. 5000) and among the most effective performance media agencies of the 2020s (Varos). Our founders were recognized by Forbes 30 Under 30 for advancing the intersection of technology, marketing, and advertising, cementing Darkroom’s role as a defining player in the future of media innovation.

This is a fully remote role supporting a team in the EST time zone and is open to candidates based in Portugal, Spain and Brazil.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Key Responsibilities:

 - Develop custom Shopify themes and functionalities from scratch, ensuring responsive design, cross-browser compatibility, and optimal site performance.

 - Implement third-party Shopify apps and integrations to enhance store functionality.

 - Write clean, maintainable, and efficient code, adhering to best practices.

 - Collaborate with design and marketing teams to translate UI/UX designs into engaging and responsive websites.

 - Maintain, debug, and improve existing Shopify sites, identifying areas for optimization.

 - Manage backend tasks such as product catalog updates, customer data integrations, payment gateway setups, and other custom integrations.

 - Optimize sites for speed, scalability, SEO, and conversions.

 - Conduct comprehensive QA and testing processes before deployment.

 - Stay updated on emerging Shopify trends, tools, and technologies, and proactively suggest improvements.

You Should Apply if this Sounds Like You:

 - 3+ years of hands-on experience developing Shopify websites.

 - Proficiency in Liquid templating language, HTML5, CSS3, and JavaScript.

 - Experience with Shopify API integrations and custom app development.

 - Knowledge of Tailwind CSS for rapid UI development.

 - Knowledge of version control systems (Git) and familiarity with modern development workflows.

 - Strong understanding of website optimization techniques, SEO best practices, and analytics.

 - Excellent problem-solving skills and ability to work independently as well as part of a team.

 - Strong communication and collaboration skills.

Preferred Qualifications:

 - Familiarity with Shopify Plus and headless commerce architectures.

 - Experience with GraphQL.

 - Experience with relevant frameworks (React, Vue.js, or similar).

 - Previous experience in an agency or fast-paced e-commerce environment.
   
   


WORKING AT DARKROOM

Darkroom is not a typical agency. We are looking for A players who want to build something great. We move fast, think deeply, and expect every team member to bring insight and rigor to everything they touch. We hold a high bar for performance, creativity, and ownership - but we also support each other relentlessly. No egos, no red tape - just world-class talent building something remarkable.

We believe in autonomy with accountability, truth over comfort, and outcomes over optics.

 - Unlimited PTO + Local h

source_jobs.title: Full-Stack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHAT WE'RE BUILDING

We're empowering small teams with technology that makes it easier to market and grow businesses. Our current focus is to help consumer brands shift from "workflow automation" to "agent management" within their marketing operations. Shadow is the AI coordination layer — providing shared AI memory, centralized agent control, and model orchestration for marketing teams.

 


WHY JOIN SHADOW?

 - Product Ownership You'll ship production code daily and help steer key product and technical decisions.

 - Shape the Engineering Culture You'll influence how we work—tools, processes, standards, and hiring.

 - Work with Challenger Consumer Brands Talk directly to customers (CEOs, CMOs, VP's) of fast-growing consumer brands—some doing $80M–$500M in revenue.

 


THE AGENCY BEHIND THE PRODUCT

Shadow is built alongside Darkroom — a performance marketing agency that's been operating for 10 years, employs 100+ people, runs 100+ clients at a time, and has worked with over 1,000 consumer brands. The agency is both our proving ground and our first user, which means the data you build with is real marketing data at real volume from day one — not a synthetic demo.




THE ROLE

You'll sit squarely at the intersection of back‑end and front‑end, ensuring seamless integration between APIs, databases, UIs, and ML services. You'll design, build, and scale features end‑to‑end, especially our AI/ML‑powered experiences, while mentoring peers and driving architecture decisions.



Core Tech & Tools

 - Languages & Frameworks: Python, Node.js, React (TypeScript)

 - Datastore: PostgreSQL

 - Cloud & Infra: Google Cloud Platform, Airflow, Terraform, Docker, Kubernetes

 - ML/AI: LLMs, RAG, prompt engineering

 


WHAT YOU'LL OWN

 - Architect and implement full‑stack features, from database schema to React components, optimized for scale and reliability.

 - Build and maintain RESTful/GraphQL APIs, data pipelines, and distributed services in GCP.

 - Integrate, prompt, and debug LLMs and generative AI tools; own RAG or fine‑tuning pipelines.

 - Ensure front‑end and back‑end systems interoperate flawlessly, minimize friction, optimize data flow, and enforce contracts.

 - Collaborate with product, research, design, and infra teams to define requirements, iterate rapidly, and ship production‑grade code.

 - Monitor performance, reliability, and security.

 - Mentor junior engineers through code reviews, architecture reviews, and shared best practices.

 


MUST HAVES

 - 5+ years of professional software engineering experience with end‑to‑end ownership in a full‑stack role.

 - Deep expertise in Python, Node.js, React/TypeScript, and PostgreSQL.

 - Able to be hands‑on with GCP, containerization (Docker/K8s), and building/supporting high‑traffic systems.

 - Proven experience integrating AI/ML models (LLMs, NLP, RAG) into production apps.

 - Familiarity or strong interest in working with MCP servers.

 - Exceptional problem‑solving skills and a product mindset: you think deeply about UX, performance, and business impact.

 - You sweat both technical details and end-user experience.

 


NICE TO HAVES

 - Experience with multi‑step or agentic AI workflows.

 - Background in AI infrastructure or tooling companies.

 - Contributions to open‑source AI/ML projects.

 


WHAT WE OFFER

 - Competitive salary (roles, responsibilities, and comp grow as we do)

 - Top-tier health, vision, and dental insurance (US)

 - Regular team off-sites

 - Regular hack weeks

 


COMPENSATION

Yearly compensation for this role is $120,000. Actual compensation will be determined based on experience, skills, and qualifications. This role is also eligible for performance-based compensation. A summary of benefits is listed above.

 
 


EQUAL OPPORTUNITY STATEMENT

We are an equal opportunity workplace—we are dedicated to equal employment opportunities regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national orientation, sexual orientation, age, citizenship

source_jobs.title: Senior Data Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHAT WE'RE BUILDING

We're empowering small teams with technology that makes it easier to market and grow businesses. Our current focus is to help consumer brands shift from "workflow automation" to "agent management" within their marketing operations. Shadow is the AI coordination layer — providing shared AI memory, centralized agent control, and model orchestration for marketing teams.




WHY JOIN SHADOW?

 - Product Ownership You'll ship production code daily and help steer key product and technical decisions.

 - Shape the Engineering Culture You'll influence how we work—tools, processes, standards, and hiring.

 - Work with Challenger Consumer Brands Talk directly to customers (CEOs, CMOs, VP's) of fast-growing consumer brands—some doing $80M–$500M in revenue.




THE AGENCY BEHIND THE PRODUCT

Shadow is built alongside Darkroom — a performance marketing agency that's been operating for 10 years, employs 100+ people, runs 100+ clients at a time, and has worked with over 1,000 consumer brands. The agency is both our proving ground and our first user, which means the data you build with is real marketing data at real volume from day one — not a synthetic demo.



This is a fully remote role supporting a team in the EST time zone (9 AM–5 PM EST).




THE ROLE

You own the pipelines that bring the world's marketing data into Shadow — and keep them fast, accurate, and reliable as we scale to thousands of users. Every brand connects its full stack (ad platforms, ecommerce, analytics, email/SMS), and you make that data land cleanly, normalize into shared schemas, and stay in sync. The agent is only as good as the data underneath it; that layer is yours.

This is a hands-on, build-heavy engineering role for someone who has run large data systems before and wants to do it again in a smaller, faster environment.




WHAT YOU'LL OWN

 - Build and scale the ingestion layer across third-party marketing APIs (Meta, Google, TikTok, GA4, Shopify, Klaviyo, and more) — auth, extraction, rate-limit handling, backfill, and incremental sync.

 - Design normalization and transformation pipelines that map messy, platform-specific data into shared, queryable schemas (e.g. a unified creative/campaign/order model).

 - Own data reliability at scale — sync accuracy, freshness, coverage, and observability. Build the systems that detect when a connection breaks or a number looks wrong before a user does.

 - Engineer for multi-tenant scale and security: pipelines and storage that stay performant and cost-efficient across 1,000+ users and hundreds of connected brands — with strict data isolation, privacy, and compliance built in, not bolted on.

 - Partner with the AI and data-science teams to expose clean, well-modeled data the agent can retrieve and reason over.




MUST HAVES

 - Experience building and operating large enterprise data pipelines engineered for scale — systems serving 1,000+ users (or equivalent data volume / tenancy), where reliability, isolation, and cost at scale were real constraints you solved.

 - Strong SQL and Python, with production experience in a modern data warehouse (BigQuery, Snowflake, Redshift, or similar).

 - Deep familiarity with ETL/ELT patterns, incremental sync, schema design, and data modeling for analytics.

 - Built and maintained integrations against third-party APIs — OAuth flows, pagination, rate limits, schema drift, and the operational reality of connectors that break.

 - A bias toward observability and data quality: you instrument your pipelines and you don't ship data you can't trust.

 - Experience building or operating within SOC 2-compliant systems with enterprise-grade security and privacy — you've handled sensitive customer data under real compliance constraints (access controls, encryption, data isolation, auditability) and treat it as a first-class engineering requirement.




NICE TO HAVES

 - Experience in martech, adtech, or an adjacent data-heavy marketing domain — you've worked with ad pl

notes: Darkroom is a growth marketing firm with a mix of tech and non-tech roles. They have a strong focus on AI and commerce, and their job postings reflect this.
